title: Authors and Citation

code: @Manual{,   title = {EcoacousticUtilities: Utilities for Ecoacoustics},   author = {WK Cornwell},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://wcornwell.github.io/ecoacoustic_utilities/}, }

title: Utilities for Ecoacoustics
text: Utilities downloading analyzing ecoacoustic data iNaturalist Atlas Living Australia (ALA).

title: Utilities for Ecoacoustics
text: can install development version EcoacousticUtilities GitHub:
code: # install.packages("devtools") # devtools::install_github("wcornwell/ecoacoustic_utilities")

title: Utilities for Ecoacoustics
text: Use case: Download actual audio files specific taxon download=FALSE: Get quick count total observations sounds download=TRUE: Download N audio files local directory Use case: Download observation photos specific taxon download=FALSE: Get quick count total observations photos download=TRUE: Download N images local directory Supports image_size: “original” (default, 2048px), “large”, “medium”, “small” Metadata includes observed taxon, date, latitude, longitude Use case: Identify species recordings taxon/region Returns data.frame counts per species (taxon_id, scientific_name, common_name, n_recordings) Useful finding well-documented species downloading Use case: Create publication-ready plots species recording counts Horizontal bar chart customizable colors labels

title: Utilities for Ecoacoustics
text: Use case: Download high-quality research recordings ALA nodes Automatically handles various metadata formats unique file naming Efficiently limits searches avoid API timeouts get_ala_circle_occurrences() - Download occurrence records within circular area

title: Utilities for Ecoacoustics
text: biggest_files() - Find largest files directory find_duplicate_wavs() - Detect duplicate WAV files content hash training_dataset_summary() - Comprehensive analysis audio training datasets

title: Utilities for Ecoacoustics
code: library(EcoacousticUtilities)  # Find largest files in a directory big_files <- biggest_files("/path/to/directory", n = 20)  # Find duplicate WAV files duplicates <- find_duplicate_wavs("/path/to/audio/library")

title: Utilities for Ecoacoustics
code: # 1. Quick check: How many sound recordings exist? n_sounds <- get_inat_sounds(   "Turnix maculosus",   place_name = "Australia",   download = FALSE # Just get the count ) print(n_sounds) # e.g., 450  # 2. Which species have the most recordings? species_summary <- get_inat_species_summary(   taxon_name = "Orthoptera", # All crickets/grasshoppers   place_name = "Australia",   min_recordings = 5 # Only species with 5+ recordings ) print(species_summary)  # 3. Visualize the results plot_inat_species_summary(species_summary, top_n = 15)  # 4. Download actual audio files for a specific species get_inat_sounds(   "Turnix maculosus",   place_name = "Australia",   target_n = 100,   download = TRUE, # Download files   quality = "research" )

title: Utilities for Ecoacoustics
code: # 1. Quick check: How many observation photos exist? n_photos <- get_inat_images(   "Cacatua galerita",   place_name = "Australia",   download = FALSE # Just get the count ) print(n_photos)  # 2. Download original-size images (default) get_inat_images(   "Cacatua galerita",   place_name = "Australia",   target_n = 50,   download = TRUE,   quality = "research" ) # Files saved to images/images/, metadata in images/metadata.csv # Metadata includes: observed taxon name, date, lat, long, license, etc.  # 3. Download medium-sized images globally (no place filter) get_inat_images(   "Danaus plexippus",   use_place_filter = FALSE,   target_n = 100,   image_size = "medium" # Smaller files (~500px) )

title: Utilities for Ecoacoustics
text: ’s real example analyzing cricket grasshopper recordings Australia:
code: library(EcoacousticUtilities) #> galah: version 2.1.2 #> ℹ Default node set to ALA (ala.org.au). #> ℹ See all supported GBIF nodes with `show_all(atlases)`. #> ℹ To change nodes, use e.g. `galah_config(atlas = "GBIF")`. library(ggplot2)  # Get species with at least 5 recordings orth_summary <- get_inat_species_summary(   taxon_name = "Orthoptera",   place_name = "Australia",   min_recordings = 5 ) #> Processing page 1 (160 observations) #> No more results at page 2.  # View top species head(orth_summary, 10) #>    taxon_id         scientific_name                    common_name n_recordings #> 2    623017   Gryllotalpa pluvialis                           <NA>           26 #> 8    397920   Teleogryllus commodus Australian Black Field Cricket           18 #> 4    701931 Pseudorhynchus lessonii  Lesson's Mimicking Snout-nose           15 #> 12   894013    Hexacentrus mundurra       Fierce Predatory Katydid            9 #> 6    569917   Gryllotalpa australis          Southern Mole Cricket            6 #> 7    633763      Oecanthus angustus            Common Tree Cricket            5  # Create visualization plot_inat_species_summary(   orth_summary,   top_n = 15,   title = "Australian Orthoptera: Top Species by Sound Recordings" )

title: Utilities for Ecoacoustics
code: # Configure ALA email (required for galah) library(galah) galah_config(email = "your.email@example.com")  # 1. Download sounds for a specific species get_ala_sounds(   "Notaden bennettii",   target_n = 10,   supplier = "CSIRO", # Optional: filter for CSIRO/ANWC records   include_taxon_name = TRUE )  # 2. Get ALA occurrences within a radius birds <- get_ala_circle_occurrences(   taxon = "Aves",   lat = -33.7,   lon = 151.3,   radius_km = 10,   email = "your.email@example.com" )

title: Utilities for Ecoacoustics
code: # Analyze an audio training dataset summary <- training_dataset_summary("/path/to/audio/dataset") print(summary) # Shows class distribution, file types, sizes, durations, outliers

title: Utilities for Ecoacoustics
text: MIT © Z Cornwell

what: Analyze Call Properties: Frequency Range and Pulse Rate — analyze_call_properties
title: Analyze Call Properties: Frequency Range and Pulse Rate — analyze_call_properties
text: function calculates frequency range (min, max, bandwidth) pulse rate (pulses per second) given audio file Wave object. can process entire file specific selections defined selection table.

title: Analyze Call Properties: Frequency Range and Pulse Rate — analyze_call_properties
code: analyze_call_properties(   wave,   selection_table = NULL,   threshold = 5,   msmooth = c(50, 0),   dmin = 0.001,   auto_clean = TRUE,   plot = FALSE,   ... )

title: Analyze Call Properties: Frequency Range and Pulse Rate — analyze_call_properties
text: wave Wave object character string pointing WAV file. selection_table data frame containing selections. converting selection table format (e.g., standard `warbleR` Raven format). Must contain columns: selec, start, end. NULL (default), entire wave analyzed. threshold Numeric. Amplitude threshold (%) pulse detection (passed timer). Defaults 5. msmooth Vector length 2. Smoothing parameters amplitude envelope (window length samples, overlap %). Passed timer. Defaults c(50, 0) (approx 1ms 44.1kHz), optimized rapid pulses/trills. dmin Numeric. Minimum duration (seconds) pulse detected. Passed timer. Defaults 0.001 (1ms). auto_clean Logical. TRUE (default), function applies bandpass filter (using detected frequency range) audio detecting pulses. substantially improves robustness noise. plot Logical. TRUE, plots oscillogram detected pulses. Defaults FALSE. ... Additional arguments passed timer specprop.

title: Analyze Call Properties: Frequency Range and Pulse Rate — analyze_call_properties
text: data frame following columns: file: Name file "WaveObject". selec: Selection ID ("" selection table). duration: Duration selection seconds. freq_peak: Peak/Dominant frequency (kHz) (frequency maximum amplitude). freq_min: Minimum frequency (kHz) (5th percentile distribution). freq_max: Maximum frequency (kHz) (95th percentile distribution). bandwidth: Bandwidth (kHz) (freq_max - freq_min). pulse_rate: Pulses per second (detected pulses / duration). num_pulses: Total number detected pulses.

title: Analyze Call Properties: Frequency Range and Pulse Rate — analyze_call_properties
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Analyze a file res <- analyze_call_properties("path/to/file.wav")  # Analyze with a selection table sels <- data.frame(selec = 1, start = 0, end = 1) res_sel <- analyze_call_properties("path/to/file.wav", selection_table = sels) } # }

what: Find the Largest Files in a Directory — biggest_files
title: Find the Largest Files in a Directory — biggest_files
text: Recursively searches directory returns N largest files size, human-readable size formatting.

title: Find the Largest Files in a Directory — biggest_files
code: biggest_files(root_dir, n = 10, pattern = NULL, ignore_case = TRUE)

title: Find the Largest Files in a Directory — biggest_files
text: root_dir Character. Path root directory search. Must exist. n Numeric. Number largest files return. Default 10. pattern Character. Optional regex pattern filter files name. Default NULL (filtering). ignore_case Logical. Whether pattern matching case-insensitive. Default TRUE.

title: Find the Largest Files in a Directory — biggest_files
text: data.frame columns: size_human Human-readable file size (e.g., "1.5 MB") size File size bytes mtime Last modification time POSIXct path Full file path Returns empty data.frame files found.

title: Find the Largest Files in a Directory — biggest_files
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Find 20 largest files in a directory big_files <- biggest_files("/path/to/directory", n = 20)  # Find largest WAV files big_wavs <- biggest_files(   "/path/to/audio",   n = 10,   pattern = "\\.wav$" ) } # }

what: Convert audio files to WAV format — convert_to_wav
title: Convert audio files to WAV format — convert_to_wav
text: function converts audio files (MP3, M4A, etc.) WAV format using `av` package system `ffmpeg` installation. useful resolving compatibility issues bioacoustic software like BirdNET may struggle compressed audio headers.

title: Convert audio files to WAV format — convert_to_wav
code: convert_to_wav(   path,   out_dir = NULL,   recursive = FALSE,   delete_original = FALSE )

title: Convert audio files to WAV format — convert_to_wav
text: path Character. Path single audio file directory containing audio files. out_dir Character. Optional. Directory save converted WAV files. Defaults directory input. recursive Logical. function search audio files recursively? Defaults FALSE. delete_original Logical. original files deleted successful conversion? Defaults FALSE.

title: Convert audio files to WAV format — convert_to_wav
text: Character vector paths converted WAV files.

title: Convert audio files to WAV format — convert_to_wav
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ convert_to_wav("path/to/audio_file.mp3") convert_to_wav("path/to/audio_dir", recursive = TRUE) } # }

what: Find Duplicate WAV Files by Content Hash — find_duplicate_wavs
title: Find Duplicate WAV Files by Content Hash — find_duplicate_wavs
text: Searches directory immediate subdirectories duplicate WAV files based MD5 hash file contents. Returns one representative file path set duplicates found.

title: Find Duplicate WAV Files by Content Hash — find_duplicate_wavs
code: find_duplicate_wavs(dir, parallel = TRUE, n_cores = NULL)

title: Find Duplicate WAV Files by Content Hash — find_duplicate_wavs
text: dir Character. Path directory search. Must exist. parallel Logical. TRUE, use parallel processing hashing. Default TRUE. n_cores Integer. Number cores use. Default NULL (auto-detect).

title: Find Duplicate WAV Files by Content Hash — find_duplicate_wavs
text: named character vector : Names MD5 hashes duplicate file contents Values representative file paths (first occurrence) duplicate set Returns empty character vector duplicates found.   Returns NULL invisibly WAV files found.

title: Find Duplicate WAV Files by Content Hash — find_duplicate_wavs
text: function searches specified directory immediate subdirectories (one level deep) WAV files. File matching case-insensitive. Duplicates identified computing MD5 hashes binary file contents. Requires digest package installed.

title: Find Duplicate WAV Files by Content Hash — find_duplicate_wavs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Find duplicate WAV files dups <- find_duplicate_wavs("/path/to/audio/library")  # Print duplicate sets if (length(dups) > 0) {   print(dups) } } # }

what: Download ALA Occurrences for a Taxon Within a Circular Area — get_ala_circle_occurrences
title: Download ALA Occurrences for a Taxon Within a Circular Area — get_ala_circle_occurrences
text: Retrieves occurrence records Atlas Living Australia (ALA) specified taxon within circular geographic area defined center point radius.

title: Download ALA Occurrences for a Taxon Within a Circular Area — get_ala_circle_occurrences
code: get_ala_circle_occurrences(taxon, lat, lon, radius_km = 10, email = NULL, ...)

title: Download ALA Occurrences for a Taxon Within a Circular Area — get_ala_circle_occurrences
text: taxon Character. Scientific common name ALA can resolve. lat Numeric. Latitude circle centre decimal degrees (WGS84). lon Numeric. Longitude circle centre decimal degrees (WGS84). radius_km Numeric. Radius kilometres. Default 10 km. email Character. ALA/GBIF login email. NULL, assumes already called galah_config() elsewhere. Default NULL. ... Additional arguments passed atlas_occurrences(), filter = galah_filter(year >= 2000).

title: Download ALA Occurrences for a Taxon Within a Circular Area — get_ala_circle_occurrences
text: tibble occurrence records ALA.

title: Download ALA Occurrences for a Taxon Within a Circular Area — get_ala_circle_occurrences
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Get all bird records within 10 km of a point birds <- get_ala_circle_occurrences(   taxon = "Aves",   lat = -33.7,   lon = 151.3,   radius_km = 10,   email = "your.email@example.com" )  # With additional filters recent_birds <- get_ala_circle_occurrences(   taxon = "Manorina melanocephala",   lat = -33.7,   lon = 151.3,   radius_km = 5,   filter = galah_filter(year >= 2010) ) } # }

what: Download Sound Recordings from Atlas of Living Australia (ALA) — get_ala_sounds
title: Download Sound Recordings from Atlas of Living Australia (ALA) — get_ala_sounds
text: Queries ALA sound recordings specified taxon downloads . function improves upon basic media downloads handling metadata limiting download counts.

title: Download Sound Recordings from Atlas of Living Australia (ALA) — get_ala_sounds
code: get_ala_sounds(   taxon_name,   target_n = 50,   download = TRUE,   out_dir = "sounds",   include_taxon_name = TRUE,   supplier = c("all", "CSIRO"),   as_wav = FALSE )

title: Download Sound Recordings from Atlas of Living Australia (ALA) — get_ala_sounds
text: taxon_name Character. Scientific common name taxon search . target_n Numeric. Target number sound files download. Default 50. download Logical. TRUE, downloads audio files. FALSE, returns count available recordings. Default TRUE. out_dir Character. Output directory downloaded files. create subdirectories "audio" "metadata.csv". Default "sounds". include_taxon_name Logical. TRUE, prepends taxon name filename. Default TRUE. supplier Character. Data supplier filter . Options "" "CSIRO". "CSIRO", filters records institutionCode "ANWC" collectionCode "Sounds". lightweight count check performed first; CSIRO records exist, function returns 0 immediately without downloading full media metadata. Default "". as_wav Logical. TRUE, converts downloaded audio files WAV format. Default FALSE.

title: Download Sound Recordings from Atlas of Living Australia (ALA) — get_ala_sounds
text: Character. absolute path metadata CSV file created/updated.

title: Download Sound Recordings from Atlas of Living Australia (ALA) — get_ala_sounds
text: download = TRUE, function: Creates out_dir/audio/ directory audio files Creates out_dir/metadata_ala.csv record metadata Downloads files named [Taxon_Name_]<record_id>.<ext> Deduplicates media records URL downloading (ALA can return     multiple rows pointing audio file) Skips files already exist output directory     downloaded current batch Uses ALA recordID filenames; missing, extracts     UUID audio URL traceability back source record Falls back searched taxon_name scientificName     missing record Automatically converts recordings WAV as_wav = TRUE

title: Download Sound Recordings from Atlas of Living Australia (ALA) — get_ala_sounds
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ galah_config(email = "your-email@example.com") # Search and download up to 10 sounds as WAV get_ala_sounds("Teleogryllus commodus", target_n = 10, as_wav = TRUE) } # }

title: Download Images from iNaturalist — get_inat_images
text: Queries iNaturalist API find optionally download photos specified taxon. Can filter geographic location observation quality.

title: Download Images from iNaturalist — get_inat_images
code: get_inat_images(   taxon_name,   place_name = "Australia",   target_n = 300,   download = TRUE,   out_dir = "images",   allowed_licenses = tolower(c("cc0", "cc-by", "cc-by-sa", "cc-by-nc", "cc-by-nc-sa")),   use_place_filter = FALSE,   quality = c("research", "all"),   include_taxon_name = TRUE,   image_size = c("original", "large", "medium", "small") )

title: Download Images from iNaturalist — get_inat_images
text: taxon_name Character. Scientific common name taxon search . place_name Character. Name geographic place filter . Required use_place_filter = TRUE. Default "Australia". target_n Numeric. Target number image files download. Default 300. download Logical. TRUE, downloads image files. FALSE, returns count available photos. Default TRUE. out_dir Character. Output directory downloaded files. create subdirectories "images" "metadata.csv". Default "images". allowed_licenses Character vector. Lowercase license codes accept. Default includes Creative Commons licenses: cc0, cc-, cc--sa, cc--nc, cc--nc-sa. Empty license codes also accepted. use_place_filter Logical. TRUE, filters observations place. FALSE, searches globally, UNLESS place_name explicitly provided. Default FALSE. quality Character. Quality grade filter: "research" research-grade observations , "" quality grades. Default "research". include_taxon_name Logical. TRUE, prepends taxon name filename (e.g., "Genus_species_ObsID_PhotoID.ext"). Default TRUE. image_size Character. Size image download. One "original" (2048px), "large" (1024px), "medium" (500px), "small" (240px). Default "original".

title: Download Images from iNaturalist — get_inat_images
text: Integer. Total number matching records found iNaturalist.

title: Download Images from iNaturalist — get_inat_images
text: download = TRUE, function: Creates out_dir/images/ directory image files Creates out_dir/metadata.csv observation metadata Downloads files named [Taxon_Name_]ObsID_PhotoID.ext Skips files already exist output directory Respects API rate limits 1.1 second delays requests iNaturalist stores photos several predefined sizes. default URL returned API uses "square" (75px) size. function replaces size component URL requested image_size.

title: Download Images from iNaturalist — get_inat_images
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Check how many photos are available n <- get_inat_images(     "Cacatua galerita",     place_name = "Australia",     download = FALSE )  # Download up to 300 research-grade photos get_inat_images(     "Cacatua galerita",     place_name = "Australia",     target_n = 300,     download = TRUE,     quality = "research" )  # Global search with medium-sized images get_inat_images(     "Danaus plexippus",     use_place_filter = FALSE,     target_n = 100,     download = TRUE,     image_size = "medium" ) } # }

what: Download Sound Recordings from iNaturalist — get_inat_sounds
title: Download Sound Recordings from iNaturalist — get_inat_sounds
text: Queries iNaturalist API find optionally download sound recordings specified taxon. Can filter geographic location observation quality.

title: Download Sound Recordings from iNaturalist — get_inat_sounds
code: get_inat_sounds(   taxon_name,   place_name = "Australia",   target_n = 300,   download = TRUE,   out_dir = "sounds",   allowed_licenses = tolower(c("cc0", "cc-by", "cc-by-sa", "cc-by-nc", "cc-by-nc-sa")),   use_place_filter = FALSE,   quality = c("research", "all"),   include_taxon_name = TRUE,   as_wav = FALSE )

title: Download Sound Recordings from iNaturalist — get_inat_sounds
text: taxon_name Character. Scientific common name taxon search . place_name Character. Name geographic place filter . Required use_place_filter = TRUE. Default "Australia". target_n Numeric. Target number sound files download. Default 300. download Logical. TRUE, downloads audio files. FALSE, returns count available recordings. Default TRUE out_dir Character. Output directory downloaded files. create subdirectories "audio" "metadata.csv". Default "sounds". allowed_licenses Character vector. Lowercase license codes accept. Default includes Creative Commons licenses: cc0, cc-, cc--sa, cc--nc, cc--nc-sa. Empty license codes also accepted. use_place_filter Logical. TRUE, filters observations place. FALSE, searches globally, UNLESS place_name explicitly provided. Default FALSE. quality Character. Quality grade filter: "research" research-grade observations , "" quality grades. Default "research". include_taxon_name Logical. TRUE, prepends taxon name filename (e.g., "Genus_species_ObsID_SoundID.ext"). Default TRUE. as_wav Logical. TRUE, converts downloaded audio files WAV format. Default FALSE.

title: Download Sound Recordings from iNaturalist — get_inat_sounds
text: Integer. Total number matching records found iNaturalist.

title: Download Sound Recordings from iNaturalist — get_inat_sounds
text: download = TRUE, function: Creates out_dir/audio/ directory audio files Creates out_dir/metadata.csv observation metadata Downloads files named [Taxon_Name_]ObsID_SoundID.ext Skips files already exist output directory Respects API rate limits 1.1 second delays requests Automatically converts recordings WAV as_wav = TRUE

title: Download Sound Recordings from iNaturalist — get_inat_sounds
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Check how many recordings are available n <- get_inat_sounds(   "Turnix maculosus",   place_name = "Australia",   download = FALSE )  # Download up to 300 research-grade recordings get_inat_sounds(   "Turnix maculosus",   place_name = "Australia",   target_n = 300,   download = TRUE,   quality = "research" )  # Global search (no place filter) get_inat_sounds(   "Gryllus bimaculatus",   use_place_filter = FALSE,   target_n = 100,   download = TRUE,   as_wav = TRUE ) } # }

what: Get iNaturalist Species Summary by Sound Recording Counts — get_inat_species_summary
title: Get iNaturalist Species Summary by Sound Recording Counts — get_inat_species_summary
text: Queries iNaturalist API find species-level taxa sound recordings meet specified criteria. Counts valid sound recordings per species based license requirements.

title: Get iNaturalist Species Summary by Sound Recording Counts — get_inat_species_summary
code: get_inat_species_summary(   min_recordings = 1,   taxon_name = NULL,   taxon_id = NULL,   place_name = "Australia",   place_id = NULL,   quality_grade = "research",   per_page = 200,   max_pages = 10,   allowed_licenses = tolower(c("cc0", "cc-by", "cc-by-sa", "cc-by-nc", "cc-by-nc-sa")) )

title: Get iNaturalist Species Summary by Sound Recording Counts — get_inat_species_summary
text: min_recordings Numeric. Minimum number valid recordings required include species results. Default 1. taxon_name Character. Optional taxon name resolve ID. provided, overrides taxon_id. Default NULL. taxon_id Integer. iNaturalist taxon ID search within. Default NULL (defaults 47651 Orthoptera taxon_id taxon_name NULL). Ignored taxon_name provided. place_name Character. Name place search within. Default "Australia". place_id Integer. Optional iNaturalist place ID. NULL, resolved place_name. Default NULL. quality_grade Character. Quality grade filter observations. Default "research". per_page Integer. Number results per API page. Default 200. max_pages Numeric. Maximum number pages retrieve. Default 10. Use Inf available pages. allowed_licenses Character vector. Lowercase license codes accept. Default includes Creative Commons licenses: cc0, cc-, cc--sa, cc--nc, cc--nc-sa.

title: Get iNaturalist Species Summary by Sound Recording Counts — get_inat_species_summary
text: data.frame columns: taxon_id, scientific_name, common_name,   n_recordings. Sorted n_recordings descending order. Returns empty   data.frame species meet criteria.

title: Get iNaturalist Species Summary by Sound Recording Counts — get_inat_species_summary
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Get Orthoptera species with at least 5 recordings in Australia orth_aus <- get_inat_species_summary(   min_recordings = 5,   place_name = "Australia" )  # Search by taxon name instead of ID crickets <- get_inat_species_summary(   taxon_name = "Gryllidae",   min_recordings = 5,   place_name = "United States" ) } # }

what: Check if a remote API is reachable — is_api_reachable
title: Check if a remote API is reachable — is_api_reachable
text: Check remote API reachable

title: Check if a remote API is reachable — is_api_reachable
code: is_api_reachable(api_type = c("inat", "ala"), timeout_secs = 2)

title: Check if a remote API is reachable — is_api_reachable
text: api_type Character. Either "inat" "ala". timeout_secs Numeric. Seconds wait timing . Default 2.

title: Check if a remote API is reachable — is_api_reachable
text: Logical. TRUE reachable, FALSE otherwise.

title: Plot iNaturalist Species Summary — plot_inat_species_summary
text: Creates informative bar plot showing number sound recordings per species output get_inat_species_summary().

title: Plot iNaturalist Species Summary — plot_inat_species_summary
code: plot_inat_species_summary(   species_summary,   top_n = 20,   use_common_names = TRUE,   color = "#2E86AB",   title = NULL )

title: Plot iNaturalist Species Summary — plot_inat_species_summary
text: species_summary Data.frame. Output get_inat_species_summary(). Must contain columns: scientific_name (common_name) n_recordings. top_n Integer. Number top species display. Default 20. use_common_names Logical. TRUE common_name column exists, use common names instead scientific names. Default TRUE. color Character. Bar fill color. Default "#2E86AB" (blue). title Character. Plot title. NULL, generates automatic title. Default NULL.

title: Plot iNaturalist Species Summary — plot_inat_species_summary
text: ggplot2 object can customized saved.

title: Plot iNaturalist Species Summary — plot_inat_species_summary
text: function creates horizontal bar plot species names y-axis recording counts x-axis. Species ordered recording count (highest top). species common name, scientific name used fallback.

title: Plot iNaturalist Species Summary — plot_inat_species_summary
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Get species summary summary <- get_inat_species_summary(   taxon_name = "Orthoptera",   place_name = "Australia",   min_recordings = 50 )  # Plot top 15 species plot_inat_species_summary(summary, top_n = 15)  # Use scientific names and custom color plot_inat_species_summary(   summary,   top_n = 10,   use_common_names = FALSE,   color = "#E63946" )  # Save plot p <- plot_inat_species_summary(summary) ggsave("species_summary.png", p, width = 10, height = 8) } # }

what: Skip test if API is unreachable — skip_if_api_unavailable
title: Skip test if API is unreachable — skip_if_api_unavailable
text: Helper testthat elegantly skip integration tests APIs .

title: Skip test if API is unreachable — skip_if_api_unavailable
code: skip_if_api_unavailable(api_type)

title: Skip test if API is unreachable — skip_if_api_unavailable
text: api_type Character. "inat" "ala".

title: Summarize Audio Training Dataset — training_dataset_summary
text: Analyzes directory structure containing audio files (typically organized class subdirectories) provides comprehensive statistics including class distribution, file types, duration statistics, file sizes, outlier detection.

title: Summarize Audio Training Dataset — training_dataset_summary
code: training_dataset_summary(   root_dir,   audio_extensions = c("wav", "mp3", "flac", "m4a", "ogg", "aiff", "aif"),   outlier_threshold = 3,   use_tuneR = TRUE,   ignore_classes = NULL,   sample_size = NULL,   parallel = TRUE,   n_cores = NULL,   show_progress = TRUE )

title: Summarize Audio Training Dataset — training_dataset_summary
text: root_dir Character. Path root directory containing audio files. Typically organized subdirectories representing different classes. audio_extensions Character vector. File extensions consider audio files. Default includes common formats: wav, mp3, flac, m4a, ogg, aiff. outlier_threshold Numeric. Number standard deviations mean flag outliers. Default 3. use_tuneR Logical. TRUE tuneR package available, extract detailed audio metadata (duration, sample rate, etc.). Default TRUE. ignore_classes Character vector. Names class subdirectories ignore (e.g. "noise"). Default NULL. sample_size Integer. NULL, randomly sample many files duration analysis instead processing files. Useful large datasets. Default NULL (process files). parallel Logical. TRUE, use parallel processing audio metadata extraction (requires parallel package). Default TRUE. n_cores Integer. Number cores use parallel processing. NULL, uses half available cores. Default NULL. show_progress Logical. TRUE, show progress bar audio metadata extraction. Default TRUE.

title: Summarize Audio Training Dataset — training_dataset_summary
text: list following components: summary Data.frame overall statistics class_distribution Data.frame per-class file counts file_type_distribution Data.frame counts file extension duration_stats Data.frame duration statistics (available) size_stats Data.frame file size statistics outliers Data.frame flagged unusual files imbalance_stats Data.frame class imbalance metrics recommendations Character vector transfer learning advice

title: Summarize Audio Training Dataset — training_dataset_summary
text: function recursively scans directory structure : Counts files per subdirectory (classes) Identifies file types extension Calculates file size distributions Extracts audio duration (tuneR available) Flags outliers based size duration Outliers identified files deviate mean outlier_threshold standard deviations either size duration.

title: Summarize Audio Training Dataset — training_dataset_summary
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Analyze a training dataset summary <- training_dataset_summary("/path/to/audio/dataset")  # Ignore noise class summary <- training_dataset_summary(   "/path/to/dataset",   ignore_classes = c("noise", "background") )  # Print summary statistics print(summary$summary) print(summary$class_distribution)  # Check for outliers if (nrow(summary$outliers) > 0) {   print(summary$outliers) }  # For large datasets, use sampling for faster analysis summary <- training_dataset_summary(   "/path/to/large/dataset",   sample_size = 1000,  # Only analyze 1000 random files   parallel = TRUE,     # Use parallel processing   n_cores = 4          # Use 4 CPU cores )  # Skip duration extraction for even faster analysis summary <- training_dataset_summary(   "/path/to/dataset",   use_tuneR = FALSE  # Only file sizes, no duration data ) } # }
